authoritative_sources.title: RFC 3261 - SIP: Session Initiation Protocol
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF standards-track SIP specification is the authoritative baseline for signaling requests, responses, transactions, dialogs, proxies, registrars, and error semantics used in SIP trunks and UC systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SIP signaling evidence intake, call setup triage, SBC ingress review
practical_use_in_pack: signaling firewall and SBC boundary scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 3263 - Session Initiation Protocol (SIP): Locating SIP Servers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF SIP server location standard defines DNS SRV/NAPTR behavior, transport selection, and failover expectations for registrar/proxy discovery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DNS SRV/NAPTR intake, registrar discovery troubleshooting, voice edge failover review

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 3264 - An Offer/Answer Model with SDP
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF offer/answer standard is authoritative for interpreting codec, media address, port, direction, and negotiation evidence in SIP/SDP troubleshooting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SDP evidence review, codec/media negotiation triage, one-way audio investigation
practical_use_in_pack: confidence limits for missing SDP evidence

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 8866 - SDP: Session Description Protocol
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Current IETF SDP specification defines session and media attributes used to interpret SIP/UC media negotiation evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SDP artifact interpretation, media attribute review, packet-flow troubleshooting

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 3550 - RTP: A Transport Protocol for Real-Time Applications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RTP standard defines sequence, timestamp, jitter, packet loss, and RTCP evidence used for voice media quality review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: RTP evidence intake, jitter/loss triage, one-way audio and media reset analysis

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 3711 - The Secure Real-time Transport Protocol (SRTP)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF SRTP standard defines secure media confidentiality, authentication, and replay protection expectations for protected RTP paths.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: secure media review, SRTP evidence handling, privacy and security caveat assessment

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 4594 - Configuration Guidelines for DiffServ Service Classes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF DiffServ guidance defines service class treatment used to constrain voice QoS, DSCP, queuing, jitter, loss, and latency recommendations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: QoS policy review, RTP performance triage, WAN tradeoff analysis

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 2131 - Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF DHCP standard defines lease and option behavior that governs address assignment, gateway, resolver, and voice endpoint provisioning assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DHCP scope and relay evidence review, voice VLAN migration triage

authoritative_sources.title: Plan Direct Routing
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official Teams Direct Routing guidance defines supported SBC integration, routing, and tenant prerequisites for a major UC platform.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: platform-specific SBC topology review, Teams Direct Routing prerequisite validation

authoritative_sources.title: Plan for media bypass with Direct Routing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official media bypass guidance defines network, firewall, public IP, and media path assumptions directly used in UC edge design.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: media bypass prerequisite review, firewall and media-path validation

authoritative_sources.title: Monitor and improve call quality for Microsoft Teams
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official guidance defines call-quality monitoring, QoS, and diagnostic evidence for Teams voice and meeting media quality.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: call-quality monitoring intake, QoS review, incident evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: Enterprise QoS Solution Reference Network Design Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Cisco official network design guide provides recognized enterprise QoS operating guidance for classification, marking, queuing, and voice/video/data treatment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: enterprise QoS design review, WAN media-quality tradeoff, operational readiness

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-58 - Security Considerations for Voice Over IP Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST public VoIP security guidance directly addresses VoIP architecture, segmentation, confidentiality, availability, emergency service, and operational risks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: voice edge security review, segmentation control, availability and emergency-service caveat handling

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-52 Rev. 2 - Guidelines for TLS Implementations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST TLS guidance constrains secure SIP/TLS signaling and certificate posture recommendations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SIP/TLS control review, certificate evidence handling, secure signaling caveats

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-53 Rev. 5 - Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST control catalog is a recognized public control baseline for audit/accountability, boundary protection, configuration management, incident response, contingency planning, monitoring, and privacy evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control objective mapping, evidence traceability, hold/release review, audit trail handling
practical_use_in_pack: control evidence and audit trail requirements

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-61 Rev. 3 - Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST current incident response guidance provides public workflow expectations for preparation, detection, analysis, response, communications, and improvement evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: call-failure incident handling, repeat defect escalation, corrective action review

authoritative_sources.title: VoIP and 911 Service
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FCC public guidance is authoritative for identifying emergency service considerations around interconnected VoIP; the specialist uses it only to mark authority boundaries and evidence needs, not to provide legal interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: emergency-call path caveat intake, safety boundary review, legal authority escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Kari's Law and RAY BAUM'S Act
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FCC public guidance establishes MLTS emergency-call and dispatchable-location context that constrains autonomous voice-network recommendations and requires authorized review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: emergency-call and dispatchable-location boundary review, release-control caveat handling

authoritative_sources.title: Current committed package baseline for voice-and-unified-communications-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Repository-owned baseline defines prior supported tasks, common inputs, expected outputs, and refusal rules that this refreshed spec pack preserves where still valid and supersedes as canonical source of truth.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: legacy boundary comparison, task preservation, stale assumption replacement
practical_use_in_pack: preserved SIP/SBC, DNS/DHCP, QoS, failover, and troubleshooting focus
legacy scenario minimum retained and exceeded
refusal rules refined into explicit spec-first boundaries

workflow_stages.name: voice-network evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Collect request intent, voice/UC scope, SIP/SBC topology, trunk inventory, DNS SRV/NAPTR, DHCP/IPAM, firewall/NAT, route, QoS, monitoring, call records, packet captures, incident/change records, and approval context before drafting final guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: control and inspection review
workflow_stages.description: Validate signaling, media transport, DNS discovery, DHCP addressing, firewall/NAT exposure, TLS/SRTP posture, QoS treatment, emergency-call path dependencies, source freshness, and tenant policy against acceptance criteria.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: fault isolation and incident handling
workflow_stages.description: Use SIP traces, SDP offers/answers, RTP/SRTP metrics, packet captures, flow logs, SBC alarms, DNS/DHCP records, route state, and CDRs to separate signaling-plane, media-plane, platform, endpoint, identity, and application ownership.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: hold, release, corrective-action, or handoff disposition
workflow_stages.description: Prepare quality review notes, hold or limited-release recommendations, control summaries, corrective-action escalations, and orchestrator handoffs without clearing holds, certifying compliance, commanding incidents, or approving production changes.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: boundary, refusal, and materialization governance
workflow_stages.description: Reject adjacent endpoint, identity, application, procurement, legal, product-selection, and approval work; record tenant adaptation and future materialization requirements while runtime remains specification-based.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: prioritized remediation plan with confidence and risk
troubleshooting packet-flow interpretation and next actions
hold, blocked-release, limited-release, or release-prerequisite recommendation
SIP/SBC topology diagram and trunk inventory
SIP traces, SDP offers/answers, RTP/SRTP packet captures, RTCP or call-quality telemetry, CDRs, SBC alarms, and call setup failure records
firewall rule exports, NAT policy, route excerpts, load-balancer listener and health-check details, flow logs, and packet captures
DNS SRV/NAPTR/A/AAAA records, resolver evidence, TTL strategy, DHCP scopes/options, relay policies, IPAM records, and voice VLAN/subnet maps
call routing policy outputs, call admission records, PSTN constraints, emergency-call path evidence, change tickets, release holds, exception records, and approval matrix
deployment, rollback, tenant adaptation, materialization, and commercialization notes
systems_of_record: SBC, voice gateway, SIP trunk provider portal, PSTN carrier portal, UC tenant admin records, and call routing policy repository
DNS authoritative zone system, resolver logs, DHCP server, DHCP relay configuration, IPAM, VLAN/subnet source of truth, and network CMDB
firewall manager, NAT policy repository, route repository, load-balancer configuration, QoS policy repository, and network source of truth
packet-capture store, flow-log store, SIP trace repository, CDR/call analytics system, call-quality dashboard, monitoring and alerting platform, and log archive
incident management, change management, release gate, quality hold, exception register, risk register, evidence repository, tenant policy repository, and approval matrix
legal/compliance and emergency-call owner records used only for routing and authority boundaries, not legal interpretation

allowed: Recommend SIP/SBC topology, segmentation, firewall/NAT posture, secure signaling/media assumptions, DNS/DHCP prerequisites, QoS treatment, call admission, failover, monitoring, and rollback gates.
Interpret SIP, SDP, RTP, SRTP, DNS, DHCP, firewall, route, flow-log, packet-capture, CDR, call-quality, and SBC evidence for troubleshooting and quality review.
Produce evidence-backed quality review notes, control summaries, hold or limited-release recommendations, corrective-action escalations, and orchestrator handoff packages.
Define materialization prerequisites, tenant adaptation constraints, refusal triggers, logging expectations, and trusted-build promotion criteria.
escalate: Required topology, trunk, firewall, DNS, DHCP, route, packet, call-quality, emergency-call, owner, release, rollback, or approval evidence is missing or contradictory.
Official platform guidance, public standards, tenant policy, emergency-call procedures, change freeze, contractual obligations, or source-of-truth records conflict without precedence.
A repeat defect, incident, nonconformance, failed release gate, or potential emergency-call impact needs formal review or corrective-action ownership.
The request requires licensed, legal, compliance, procurement, product-selection, production execution, final release, incident-command, customer-notification, or risk-acceptance authority.
refuse: Endpoint OS configuration, endpoint agent install, desk-phone firmware lifecycle, endpoint-only client tuning, or endpoint dial-plan scripting without network relevance.
Identity governance, directory administration, authentication policy, user provisioning, or application code/API implementation.
Legal interpretation, 911 compliance certification, contract drafting, carrier negotiation, license procurement, product selection, or final approval.
Bypass of documented monitoring, logging, emergency-call validation, hold, release, change-control, quality, safety, or exception boundaries.

domain_failure_modes: SIP signaling succeeds or fails for reasons unrelated to RTP media path, causing wrong-plane remediation.
SDP advertises unreachable media address or wrong direction attribute, producing one-way audio despite successful call setup.
RTP jitter, loss, reordering, NAT timeout, or DSCP remarking degrades call quality while generic network reachability appears healthy.
DNS SRV/NAPTR, resolver TTL, or registrar discovery failures are mistaken for endpoint or DHCP problems.
DHCP scope, relay, gateway, or voice VLAN option errors appear as registration or call setup defects.
SBC failover, health checks, TTLs, or carrier routing behavior creates partial regional outage or failback loop.
Firewall or NAT rules overexpose SIP/RTP, break media pinholes, or mask asymmetric routing.
Emergency-call path evidence or dispatchable-location ownership is incomplete, making release or compliance claims unsafe.
Official UC platform guidance conflicts with tenant policy, change freeze, or security exceptions.
The request drifts into endpoint, identity, application, legal, procurement, product-selection, or approval authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Insufficient evidence to clear a hold or close a finding
Request to bypass a documented safety or quality control
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific emergency-call legal obligations and dispatchable-location rules
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack records public FCC guidance as an authority boundary and requires tenant legal/compliance owner review; it does not provide legal interpretation or compliance certification.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Vendor-specific SBC behavior and carrier trunk constraints
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack requires tenant/vendor/carrier evidence before platform-specific claims and escalates when official platform guidance and tenant records conflict.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact QoS markings across carrier and WAN domains
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack permits source-backed DSCP and service-class recommendations but requires tenant/carrier policy evidence before claiming end-to-end treatment.
